hero.title: Turn your academic process into a credible evidence chain
hero.description: StudyTrace helps international students organize drafts, source records, AI-use boundaries, feedback, and writing history into a review-ready appeal evidence package.

announcement.title: Academic process proof for international students

hero.tip: StudyTrace is not a detector bypass tool. It helps students present truthful, verifiable process evidence for human review.

use_panel.title: Appeal Evidence Workspace
use_panel.description: Upload your materials and turn them into evidence cards, timelines, risk explanations, and a report draft.
use_panel.upload_description: Drafts, sources, feedback, AI-use notes, screenshots, PDFs, DOCX, images, and other records.

cards.title: Draft evidence card
cards.description: Shows outlines, revision traces, file timestamps, and writing milestones.
cards.label: Process

cards.title: Citation source card
cards.description: Connects notes, quotes, references, and final claims to source records.
cards.label: Sources

cards.title: AI boundary card
cards.description: Separates allowed assistance from student-authored work and disclosure notes.
cards.label: Disclosure

workflow.label: Main workflow
workflow.title: From raw materials to an organized appeal package
workflow.description: A guided process turns scattered writing records into a structured evidence chain that can be reviewed, explained, and exported.

items.title: Upload essay / materials
items.description: Add drafts, rubrics, source PDFs, feedback, screenshots, notes, version files, and other supporting records.

items.title: Generate evidence cards
items.description: Extract process signals, citation links, AI-use declarations, feedback records, and missing evidence prompts.

items.title: Build academic timeline
items.description: Place drafts, edits, source reading, feedback, and submissions in a clear time order.

items.title: Explain review risks
items.description: Translate gaps, inconsistencies, citation issues, and AI-use ambiguity into plain-language explanations.

items.title: Export appeal report
items.description: Produce a structured report with evidence cards, timeline, risk notes, and appendix references.

evidence.title: Evidence cards designed for academic review
evidence.description: StudyTrace does not claim to prove innocence automatically. It organizes the facts a reviewer needs to understand the student's real writing process.

items.title: Writing process proof
items.description: Draft versions, outlines, comments, saved files, and edit history are grouped into process cards.

items.title: Citation source mapping
items.description: Quotes, references, notes, and source files are connected back to claims in the paper.

items.title: AI-use boundary
items.description: Students can separate grammar help, brainstorming, translation, formatting, and original writing.

items.title: Feedback and supervision
items.description: Tutor comments, supervisor feedback, peer review, and school communications are preserved as context.

items.title: Missing evidence prompts
items.description: The workspace flags thin areas so students know what to add before exporting a report.

items.title: Report-ready appendix
items.description: Each card can reference attached files and appendix items for a cleaner appeal package.

risk.title: Risk explanation without overpromising outcomes
risk.description: The product helps students understand what may look weak or unclear in their materials, then explains how those gaps can be addressed honestly.

items.title: Timeline gaps
items.description: Identify missing dates, sudden jumps between drafts, or evidence that needs a clearer sequence.

items.title: Source ambiguity
items.description: Surface uncited source notes, weak reference trails, and claims that need stronger source support.

items.title: AI-use uncertainty
items.description: Explain where AI assistance is unclear and help frame a transparent disclosure boundary.

items.title: Authorship context
items.description: Organize writing milestones and revision evidence to show how the work developed over time.

items.title: Reviewer language
items.description: Turn technical evidence into clear, respectful language suitable for an appeal narrative.

items.title: Export quality check
items.description: Review whether the report has a coherent structure before it is downloaded or shared.

faq.title: Questions students usually ask
faq.description: StudyTrace is built for evidence organization, not for evading academic integrity processes.

items.question: Is StudyTrace an AI detector bypass tool?
items.answer: No. StudyTrace helps students organize truthful process evidence, source records, AI-use boundaries, and appeal materials. It does not promise to defeat detectors or guarantee an appeal result.

items.question: What files can students upload?
items.answer: Students can add essays, drafts, PDFs, DOCX files, screenshots, source notes, rubrics, feedback records, and other materials related to the writing process.

items.question: What is an evidence card?
items.answer: An evidence card is a structured summary of one proof point, such as a draft history, a citation source trail, feedback from a tutor, or a disclosed AI-use boundary.

items.question: Can the exported report be submitted directly?
items.answer: The report is designed as an organized draft and evidence package. Students should review it carefully and adapt it to their school’s appeal procedure.

cta.title: Start building a trustworthy academic evidence chain
cta.description: Upload materials, generate evidence cards, inspect the timeline, explain risks, and export a structured appeal report.
